IDirect3DPushBuffer8::SetTexture

Used only within a IDirect3DPushBuffer8::BeginFixup/IDirect3DPushBuffer8::EndFixup bracket to modify the corresponding call to either IDirect3DDevice8::SetTexture or IDirect3DDevice8::SwitchTexture as it was recorded in the push-buffer. This method assigns a texture to a stage for a device.

HRESULT SetTexture(
  DWORD Offset,
  DWORD Stage,
  IDirect3DBaseTexture8* pTexture
);

Parameters

Offset
[in] The offset determined during the recording in the push-buffer as returned from IDirect3DDevice8::GetPushBufferOffset just before either IDirect3DDevice8::SetTexture or IDirect3DDevice8::SwitchTexture was recorded.
Stage
[in] Stage identifier to which the texture is set. Stage identifiers are zero-based. Devices can have up to four set textures, so the maximum value allowed for Stage is 3.
pTexture
[in] Pointer to an IDirect3DBaseTexture8 interface, representing the texture being set. For complex textures, such as mipmaps and cube textures, this parameter must point to the top-level surface.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is D3D_OK.

If the method fails, the return value can be D3DERR_INVALIDCALL.

Remarks

Unlike IDirect3DDevice8::SetTexture, this method does no reference counting. When the texture is no longer needed, set the texture at the appropriate stage to NULL. Failure to do this results in a memory leak.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

Push-Buffer Overview | IDirect3DDevice8::GetTexture, IDirect3DDevice8::GetTextureStageState, IDirect3DDevice8::SetTextureStageState
